rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Use implementation annexes only for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the property
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Ensure that all visible identifiers in AGREE annex blocks are valid and in scope according to the AADL architecture
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not use range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the port is an AGREE bool in scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
For input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees, use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees in implementation annexes when they reference component ports
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the property
Do not use range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the port is an AGREE bool in scope
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
